Mexican Peso - Individual Market Analysis

Mexican Peso - Individual Market Analysis

CoT signals and diagnostics for Mexican Peso futures.

As of 2026-04-14

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Spec Net %OI of 31.3%, a Spec Z-Score of -1.32, and a 4-week flow of -8.6%, this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation. (1)
  • The signal currently occupies a Balanced_Short_Bias regime characterized by a High_Tension_Low_Flags synthetic state. (1)
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Volatile; Threshold Proximity: Moderate; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Speculator Z-Scores define Crowded regimes; levels between -2 and 2 represent non-extreme market structures. (1)
  • Flow opposing Net Direction (e.g., negative flow vs positive net %OI) indicates conviction is fading. (1)
  • Price rises while Positioning Momentum falls define a bearish divergence, suggesting a rally losing sponsorship. (1)
  • Analysis utilizes Mexican Peso - Chicago Mercantile Exchange CoT data, last observed on 2026-04-14. (1)

Key Dynamics:

  • Dominant driver is the -8.6% "Long_Reduction" flow momentum affecting the 31.3% speculative net position. (1)
  • A bearish divergence is present as price rose while positioning momentum fell during the latest period. (1)
  • High positioning tension (2.56 score) arises from the widening spread between speculator and hedger z-scores. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: Speculator Z-Score falling to or below -2.0, triggering a Crowded Short regime. (1)
  • Positioning is unstable; the regime transitioned from long-biased to short-biased twice in the last month. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Positioning continues to mean-revert as speculative conviction fades without hitting extremes.
  • Upside risk: A bullish reversal triggered by price falling while positioning momentum rises. (1)
  • Downside risk: Accelerated long liquidation if global risk sentiment shifts sharply toward risk-off. (1)

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks); driven by high-velocity capital flow shifts and weekly CoT cycles. (1)
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; current z-scores are not extreme, but tension scores are rising. (1)

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs global sentiment and carry trade demand via speculative capital allocation to high-yield EM. (1)
  • Economic mechanism: Negative flow implies a rapid exit from carry trades, signaling a shift toward risk aversion. (1)
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically interacts with USD liquidity, VIX levels, and Emerging Market credit spreads. (1)

Regime Context:

  • The signal is in a state of persistent transition between balanced biases. (1)
  • Direction of change: Weakening; speculative sponsorship is retracting despite stable price action. (1)

Model Limitations:

  • CoT data contains reporting lags; positioning extremes can persist longer than fundamentally justified. (1)
  • Revision window cannot be assessed from provided content.

Data & References:

  • CME Mexican Peso CoT Report (2026-04-14). (1)
  • Influential datapoints: Speculator Z-Score (-1.32) and 4-Week Flow (-8.6). (1)
  • Additional datasets for reliability: VIX levels and Global Liquidity indicators. (1)
